description: Fix Slack bot feedback loop: the bot's own DM replies are being processed as new user messages, causing an infinite conversation loop

userStories.title: Resolve the bot's own Slack user ID at startup and skip events from it
userStories.description: As a user, I want the bot to never respond to its own messages, preventing infinite feedback loops in Slack DMs.
acceptanceCriteria: At relay startup (inside buildSlackRelayApp), call Slack API 'auth.test' using the SLACK_BOT_TOKEN to resolve the bot's own user ID
Store the bot user ID (e.g. 'U_BOTID') in a variable inside the app factory closure
In the /slack/events handler (inside processSlackEventWithConfig), if envelope.event?.user equals the bot's user ID, log info and return immediately without processing
Also keep the existing bot_id and subtype checks as secondary guards
If auth.test fails at startup, log a warning but don't crash — the bot_id/subtype checks remain as fallback
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: The Slack auth.test API (https://slack.com/api/auth.test) returns { ok: true, user_id: 'UXXXXXXXX', bot_id: 'BXXXXXXXX', ... } when called with the bot token. Call it once at startup via fetchFn('https://slack.com/api/auth.test', { headers: { authorization: 'Bearer ' + config.SLACK_BOT_TOKEN } }). Store the user_id. Then in processSlackEventWithConfig, add: if (slackUserId === botUserId) { log.info({ slackEventId }, 'Ignoring own bot message'); return; }. The botUserId must be passed to processSlackEventWithConfig as a new parameter or stored in a closure variable accessible from the event handler. IMPORTANT: The current architecture has processSlackEventWithConfig as a standalone exported function, not a closure. The cleanest approach is: (1) call auth.test in buildSlackRelayApp, (2) pass botUserId to processSlackEventWithConfig as a new optional parameter, (3) in the /slack/events handler, pass the resolved botUserId. This avoids changing the function signature in a breaking way (make it optional with default undefined, which means no filtering = backwards compatible).

userStories.title: Add tests for bot self-message filtering
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests that verify the bot never processes its own messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: event where envelope.event.user === botUserId is NOT processed (no provision call made)
Test: event where envelope.event.user !== botUserId IS processed normally
Test: event with bot_id field is still filtered (existing check preserved)
Test: event with subtype 'bot_message' is still filtered (existing check preserved)
Test: if auth.test fails at startup, bot still functions but without self-filtering (graceful degradation)
All existing tests still pass
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Add tests to apps/slack-relay/src/dedup.test.ts or create a new apps/slack-relay/src/bot-filter.test.ts. Use the buildSlackRelayApp + app.inject() pattern. Mock the fetch to intercept auth.test and return a known bot user ID. Then send events with that user ID and verify they are not processed.

userStories.title: Commit and push
acceptanceCriteria: npm run build passes (in the claw-orchestrator repo: pnpm -r build)
pnpm test passes
Changes committed and pushed
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Branch: ralph/fix-bot-feedback-loop. Build with: pnpm -r build. Test with: pnpm test.
